div.head: The Chairman of the President’s Commission (Forbes) to the Acting Secretary of State
div.p: 9. The Commission is gratified with approval received this morning.See telegram No. 3, March 14, 5 p.m., from the Acting Secretary of State, supra. It believes it can get the entire matter settled without further delay because no debated point now remains at issue. It hopes to get all necessary documents signed today.

All data necessary for survey and report have been assembled, and, unless the Commission receives orders to the contrary, it sees no good reason why it should not leave Port-au-Prince tomorrow and proceed to Guantanamo to coal U. S. S. Rochester, and arrive at Miami about daylight March 20. The Commission will then proceed direct to Washington.
